completion_criteria_checked: Independently confirmed from scripts/check-stop-conditions.ps1's own source: it does write a stop_condition_fired event via scripts/log-event.ps1 whenever any stop condition is present -- the worker's mid-task discovery is real, not speculative.

Independently confirmed from the same script's source: its approved-lane-source list does not include any string matching docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md, so it would mechanically false-flag every Path A task's promotion_basis if run directly.

The decision to revert to a no-subprocess design was independently evaluated as the right correction within this task's real boundaries -- no side-effect-free mode, refactor, or alternative structured data source exists in repo truth, and building one would have required editing a different existing script, forbidden by this task's own scope.

The delivered Handoff/Rollover panel was independently confirmed to do what it claims: no new subprocess call, only the already-established classify-routing.ps1 call remains, the panel uses only already-loaded in-memory fields, and the script still writes only the dashboard output file with no .cockpit/ mutation.
evidence_reviewed: scripts/generate-dashboard.ps1 (full file, read via remote repo access)
scripts/check-stop-conditions.ps1 (full file, read directly to independently confirm both claimed findings)
risks: Disclosed, non-blocking, and endorsed by the reviewer: the Handoff/Rollover panel intentionally mirrors only two of check-stop-conditions.ps1's four conditions (owner decision pending; attention-needed status), not doctor.ps1-issue surfacing or lane-recognition. Reviewer agrees this narrowing is correct given the full script could not be invoked safely and duplicating all its logic would have been the worse design.

The stale approved-lane-source list in scripts/check-stop-conditions.ps1 (not recognizing docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md) remains unaddressed, as disclosed -- a real gap in a different script, out of this task's scope to fix, worth a future task.

This verification was performed via the ChatGPT manual bridge (DECISION-086) with remote, read-only repo access only -- no local execution, no independent re-run of scripts/verify-handback-guardrails.ps1 or other local guardrails. Per docs/VERIFICATION_RESULT_SPEC.md, this is additive review, not a substitute for local-guardrail-based independent verification.
summary: Both of the worker's mid-task findings (check-stop-conditions.ps1's log-write side effect; its stale approved-lane-source list not recognizing docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md) were independently confirmed by reading that script's source directly, not taken on trust. The correction to a no-subprocess design was independently judged the right call given the real constraints (no safer alternative exists in repo truth, and building one was out of scope). The delivered Handoff/Rollover panel was independently confirmed to match its claims: no new subprocess call, no .cockpit/ mutation, correct use of already-loaded state. No blocker found.
next_action: Mark task complete. Phase D2 (pcc-pathD-004 through pcc-pathD-006) is now fully delivered and verified. Phase D3 (thin write-path for controls) is explicitly owner-gated per docs/PATH_A_PLAN.md section 6 and requires its own owner decision before any task is drafted.
